title: Control Panel Tab
content:
# Control Panel Tab

When you view the details of an Asset, one of the tab options is **Control Panel**.

This is a powerful dashboard for you to customize the tools and views you want to see for this Asset.

    Any setup you create here will be duplicated to all Assets with the same Asset Type.

    This makes it easy for you to setup a group of Assets with a specific Control Panel layout to suit your requirements.

## Setup

When you first view an Asset there will be no widgets shown.

To start click on **Add Widgets** or **Manage Widgets** button.

In the Manage Widgets window, you can click on the arrow of each widget to understand what they do.

To start, simply click and drag a widget on the screen.

    You can drag it anywhere on the screen and resize it.

On the right side bar you will see the widget options available.

Here you can setup the options and views you want in the widget.

    In the top right corner dropdown menu you can also reset the widget to default settings or delete it.

When ready, you can click the **Update** button or if you have not done any changes then the **Update** button will be gray, so you can just click the close icon (x).

When you have setup the Control Panel to your satisfaction, then you can click on the Back to <ASSET> link to go back to the Asset Details page, where you will now see your Control Panel.

## Widgets

There are six widgets you can choose from.

### App Parameters

This visualizes the Asset specific App Parameters.

You can choose which applications are available in this widget.

    If you select more than one Application, then they will be displayed in the widget in tabs.

### Asset Properties

This visualizes Asset Properties.

    You can not change them here. You will need to go to the Asset Management to update any Asset Property values.

You can choose which Asset Properties are shown here.

### Data Explorer

This embeds the Data Explorer Light version into a widget.

    Data Explorer Light gives you many of the features in Data Explorer.
    
    You can only see data for this Asset. It is not possible to have multiple Asset / Data Streams available on this widget.

### Kelvin Control Mode

If any Application that is running this Asset has a Control Mode option, then you will be able to set it in Open or Closed Loop mode from this Widget.

### Recommendations

You can show any recommendations in this widget for this Asset.

    You also have the option to filter the Applications, Recommendation Types and Status to limit what Recommendations are shown in this widget.

### Schedules

You can show in this widget any schedules that are set for this Asset.

    You also have the option to filter the Applications and Schedule Type to limit what Schedules are shown in this widget.

## Custom Views

You can create multiple Control Panel setups and save them as different views.

    This allows you to quickly switch between different Control Panel setups for monitoring different types of operations.

    Administrators can also create views that are accessible to all users.

### Create New View

To create a new view, click on the **Manage Widgets** button.

Setup the control panel as required.

You can read the documentation above on how to setup your [widgets here](#setup).

    As you change the layout, the Control Panel layout will be automatically saved.

    But for a widget's configuration, you must click on the **Update** button to save the changes.

When satisfied with the new Control Panel layout and features, click on the **Back to** text in the top left corner.

You will now see two buttons next to the view dropdown list.

#### Overwrite Current View

Click on the save button to overwrite the current view's Control Panel layout.

    You can not overwrite global views set by the Administrator.
    
    Only an Administration can update any global views.

#### Save as New View

Click on the drop down arrow to the right of the save text and click on **Save View as New**.

You can then give a name for your new Control Panel view.

    If you are an administrator, you will also have the option to make this Control Panel view available to all users.

### Custom Views List

Customized views will retain any changes in the **Manage Widgets** page.

    Even if you do not save your Custom View you will not lose your view setup when you navigate away and return.

    But we do advise you save it if you want to work with multiple types of views.

This allows you to easily switch between different custom views you have created.

You can see all your saved views in this drop down menu.

    If you click on the meatball menu item { width=20 height=20 }, there is an option for the View to be **Visible to all users**.

    This option is only available if you are an administrator.

There are some default views that will be displayed. These are set by the Administrator and can not be modified.

### Edit Custom View

To edit the name of a view, on the list you can click on the meatball menu item { width=20 height=20 } and select **Edit** option.

    You can not edit default view that are set by the Administrator.

    You can make changes to a default view and save it as a new View name.

### Delete Custom View

To delete a view, on the list you can click on the meatball menu item { width=20 height=20 } and select **Delete** option.

    You can not delete any default views that are set by the Administrator.

### Manage Custom Views

    This option is only available to Administrators

You can manage all your views easily in a popup. Click on the **Manage Views** Option.

And a popup will appear.

In the scope you can select the views that will be available to all users on the Kelvin Platform.

    Any global views are not editable by the user and can not be deleted.

    Users can use the global views, change it to their requirements and save it as a new view.

### Save Custom View

And when you change the Control Panel widget layout you will have the choice to overwrite the current view or save it to a new view.

    You can only save the widget layout to views.

    Changing the settings inside a widget, for example change the recommendation time filter from 24 hours to 48 hours will not trigger the **Save** button to appear.
summary: When you view the details of an Asset, one of the tab options is Control Panel. This is a powerful dashboard for you to customize the tools and views you want to see for this Asset.
